inputs.question: Show the stadium name and the number of concerts in each stadium.

inputs.question: Find the name and location of the stadiums which some concerts happened in the years of both 2014 and 2015.

joins: stadium INNER JOIN concert ON stadium.Stadium_ID = concert.Stadium_ID

sql:
SELECT DISTINCT s.Name, s.Location
FROM stadium s
INNER JOIN concert c ON s.Stadium_ID = c.Stadium_ID
WHERE c.Year = '2014'
AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM concert c2
    WHERE c2.Stadium_ID = s.Stadium_ID
    AND c2.Year = '2015'
)

inputs.question: Which models are lighter than 3500 but not built by the 'Ford Motor Company'?

JOIN car_makers ON model_list.Maker = car_makers.Id

AND car_makers.FullName != 'Ford Motor Company'

model_list JOIN cars_data ON model_list.ModelId = cars_data.Id

aggregations: GROUP BY countries.CountryId, countries.CountryName HAVING COUNT(car_makers.Id) > 3

car_makers JOIN model_list ON car_makers.Id = model_list.Maker

FROM airlines a

sql:
SELECT DISTINCT a.Airline
FROM airlines a

INTERSECT
SELECT DISTINCT a.Airline

WHERE f.SourceAirport = 'CVO'

inputs.question: Find all airlines that have flights from airport 'CVO' but not from 'APG'.

sql:
SELECT DISTINCT airlines.Airline
FROM airlines
JOIN flights ON airlines.uid = flights.Airline
JOIN airports ON flights.SourceAirport = airports.AirportCode
WHERE flights.SourceAirport = 'CVO'
AND flights.SourceAirport != 'APG'

inputs.question: Which airlines have departures from CVO but not from APG airports?

sql:
SELECT DISTINCT a.Airline
FROM airlines a
JOIN flights f ON f.Airline = a.uid
JOIN airports ap ON f.SourceAirport = ap.AirportCode
WHERE ap.AirportCode = 'CVO'
AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM flights f2
    JOIN airports ap2 ON f2.SourceAirport = ap2.AirportCode
    WHERE ap2.AirportCode = 'APG'
    AND f2.Airline = a.uid
)

SELECT players.country_code
FROM players
GROUP BY players.country_code
HAVING COUNT(players.player_id) > 50

inputs.question: Find the total number of tours for each ranking date.

sql:
SELECT SUM(rankings.tours) AS total_tours, rankings.ranking_date
FROM rankings
GROUP BY rankings.ranking_date
ORDER BY rankings.ranking_date

inputs.question: How many total tours were there for each ranking date?

sql:
SELECT SUM(rankings.tours) AS total_tours, rankings.ranking_date
FROM rankings
GROUP BY rankings.ranking_date
ORDER BY rankings.ranking_date

inputs.question: Find the number of matches happened in each year.

aggregations: COUNT(*) AS match_count GROUP BY matches.year

sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS match_count, matches.year
FROM matches
GROUP BY matches.year
ORDER BY matches.year

inputs.question: How many matches were played in each year?

sql:

FROM matches
GROUP BY year

inputs.question: Find the name and rank of the 3 youngest winners across all matches.

joins: matches INNER JOIN players ON matches.winner_id = players.player_id

sql:
SELECT players.first_name, players.last_name, matches.winner_rank
FROM matches
INNER JOIN players ON matches.winner_id = players.player_id
ORDER BY matches.winner_age ASC
LIMIT 3

inputs.question: What are the names and ranks of the three youngest winners across all matches?

sql:
SELECT winner_name, winner_rank
FROM matches
ORDER BY winner_age ASC
LIMIT 3

inputs.question: How many different winners both participated in the WTA Championships and were left handed?

joins: players JOIN matches ON players.player_id = matches.winner_id

sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT players.player_id)
FROM players
JOIN matches ON players.player_id = matches.winner_id
WHERE players.hand = 'L' AND matches.winner_hand = 'L'

inputs.question: Find the number of left handed winners who participated in the WTA Championships.

joins: matches INNER JOIN players ON matches.winner_id = players.player_id

sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM matches 
INNER JOIN players ON matches.winner_id = players.player_id 
WHERE matches.tourney_name = 'WTA Championships' 
AND matches.winner_hand = 'L'

inputs.question: Find the first name, country code and birth date of the winner who has the highest rank points in all matches.

joins: players JOIN matches ON players.player_id = matches.winner_id

sql:
SELECT players.first_name, players.country_code, players.birth_date
FROM players
JOIN matches ON players.player_id = matches.winner_id
ORDER BY matches.winner_rank_points DESC
LIMIT 1

inputs.question: What is the first name, country code, and birth date of the player with the most winner rank points across all matches?

inputs.question: How many ships ended up being 'Captured'?

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ship WHERE disposition_of_ship = 'Captured'

inputs.question: List the name and tonnage ordered by in descending alphaetical order for the names.

sql: SELECT ship.name, ship.tonnage FROM ship ORDER BY ship.name DESC

inputs.question: List the name, date and result of each battle.

sql: SELECT battle.name, battle.date, battle.result FROM battle

inputs.question: What is maximum and minimum death toll caused each time?

sql:
SELECT MAX(death.killed) AS max_killed, MIN(death.killed) AS min_killed
FROM death
JOIN ship ON death.caused_by_ship_id = ship.id
JOIN battle ON ship.lost_in_battle = battle.id

inputs.question: What is the average number of injuries caused each time?

sql:
SELECT AVG(death.injured) 
FROM ship 
JOIN death ON ship.id = death.caused_by_ship_id

inputs.question: What are the death and injury situations caused by the ship with tonnage 't'?

sql:
SELECT ship.tonnage, death.killed, death.injured
FROM ship
JOIN death ON death.caused_by_ship_id = ship.id
WHERE ship.tonnage = 't'

inputs.question: What are the name and results of the battles when the bulgarian commander is not 'Boril'

sql: SELECT battle.name, battle.result FROM battle WHERE battle.bulgarian_commander != 'Boril'

inputs.question: What are the different ids and names of the battles that lost any 'Brig' type shipes?

sql:
SELECT DISTINCT battle.id, battle.name
FROM battle
JOIN ship ON ship.lost_in_battle = battle.id
WHERE ship.ship_type = 'Brig'

inputs.question: What are the ids and names of the battles that led to more than 10 people killed in total.

sql:
SELECT battle.id, battle.name
FROM battle
JOIN ship ON ship.lost_in_battle = battle.id
JOIN death ON death.caused_by_ship_id = ship.id
GROUP BY battle.id, battle.name
HAVING SUM(death.killed) > 10

inputs.question: What is the ship id and name that caused most total injuries?

aggregations: SUM(death.injured) AS total_injuries GROUP BY ship.id, ship.name

sql:
SELECT ship.id, ship.name, SUM(death.injured) AS total_injuries
FROM ship
JOIN death ON ship.id = death.caused_by_ship_id
GROUP BY ship.id, ship.name
ORDER BY total_injuries DESC
LIMIT 1

inputs.question: What are the distinct battle names which are between bulgarian commander 'Kaloyan' and latin commander 'Baldwin I'?

sql:
SELECT DISTINCT battle.name
FROM battle
WHERE battle.bulgarian_commander = 'Kaloyan' AND battle.latin_commander = 'Baldwin I'

inputs.question: How many different results are there for the battles?

sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT result) FROM battle

inputs.question: How many battles did not lose any ship with tonnage '225'?

sql:
SELECT COUNT(battle.id)
FROM battle
JOIN ship ON ship.lost_in_battle = battle.id
WHERE ship.tonnage != '225' OR ship.tonnage IS NULL

inputs.question: List the name and date the battle that has lost the ship named 'Lettice' and the ship named 'HMS Atalanta'

sql:
SELECT battle.name, battle.date, ship.name
FROM battle
JOIN ship ON ship.lost_in_battle = battle.id
WHERE ship.name = 'Lettice' OR ship.name = 'HMS Atalanta'

inputs.question: Show names, results and bulgarian commanders of the battles with no ships lost in the 'English Channel'.

sql:
SELECT DISTINCT battle.name, battle.result, battle.bulgarian_commander
FROM battle
JOIN ship ON ship.lost_in_battle = battle.id
WHERE ship.lost_in_battle IS NULL
  AND ship.location = 'English Channel'

inputs.question: What is the semester which most student registered in? Show both the name and the id.

sql:
SELECT Semesters.semester_id, Semesters.semester_name
FROM Semesters
JOIN Student_Enrolment ON Student_Enrolment.semester_id = Semesters.semester_id
GROUP BY Semesters.semester_id, Semesters.semester_name
ORDER BY COUNT(Student_Enrolment.student_id) DESC
LIMIT 1

inputs.question: For each semester, what is the name and id of the one with the most students registered?

sql:
SELECT 
    Semesters.semester_name, 
    Semesters.semester_id, 
    Students.student_id, 
    Students.first_name, 
    Students.last_name
FROM 
    Semesters
JOIN 
    Student_Enrolment ON Student_Enrolment.semester_id = Semesters.semester_id
JOIN 
    Students ON Student_Enrolment.student_id = Students.student_id
GROUP BY 
    Semesters.semester_id, 
    Semesters.semester_name
ORDER BY 
    COUNT(Student_Enrolment.student_id) DESC
LIMIT 1

Student_Enrolment JOIN Degree_Programs ON Student_Enrolment.degree_program_id = Degree_Programs.degree_program_id
aggregations: GROUP BY Students.student_id, Students.first_name, Students.middle_name, Students.last_name

sql:
SELECT Students.first_name, Students.middle_name, Students.last_name, Students.student_id
FROM Students
JOIN Student_Enrolment ON Students.student_id = Student_Enrolment.student_id
JOIN Degree_Programs ON Student_Enrolment.degree_program_id = Degree_Programs.degree_program_id
GROUP BY Students.student_id, Students.first_name, Students.middle_name, Students.last_name
HAVING COUNT(Degree_Programs.degree_program_id) = 2

sql:

JOIN Degree_Programs ON Student_Enrolment.degree_program_id = Degree_Programs.degree_program_id

aggregations: COUNT(Student_Enrolment.student_id) AS enrolment_count GROUP BY Degree_Programs.degree_program_id, Degree_Programs.degree_summary_name

inputs.question: Which student has enrolled for the most times in any program? List the id, first name, middle name, last name, the number of enrollments and student id.

sql:
SELECT Students.student_id, Students.first_name, Students.middle_name, Students.last_name, COUNT(Student_Enrolment.student_enrolment_id) AS enrollment_count
FROM Students
JOIN Student_Enrolment ON Students.student_id = Student_Enrolment.student_id
GROUP BY Students.student_id, Students.first_name, Students.middle_name, Students.last_name
ORDER BY enrollment_count DESC
LIMIT 1

sql:

GROUP BY Students.student_id, Students.first_name, Students.middle_name, Students.last_name

inputs.question: Whose permanent address is different from his or her current address? List his or her first name.

FROM Students

JOIN Addresses AS PermanentAddr ON Students.permanent_address_id = PermanentAddr.address_id
WHERE Students.current_address_id != Students.permanent_address_id

inputs.question: Which address holds the most number of students currently? List the address id and all lines.

joins: Students JOIN Addresses ON Students.current_address_id = Addresses.address_id
aggregations: COUNT(Students.student_id) GROUP BY Addresses.address_id, Addresses.line_1, Addresses.line_2, Addresses.line_3

sql:
SELECT Addresses.address_id, Addresses.line_1, Addresses.line_2, Addresses.line_3
FROM Students
JOIN Addresses ON Students.current_address_id = Addresses.address_id
WHERE Students.date_left IS NULL
GROUP BY Addresses.address_id, Addresses.line_1, Addresses.line_2, Addresses.line_3
ORDER BY COUNT(Students.student_id) DESC
LIMIT 1

inputs.question: Find the semester when both Master students and Bachelor students got enrolled in.

joins: Student_Enrolment JOIN Degree_Programs ON Student_Enrolment.degree_program_id = Degree_Programs.degree_program_id
Student_Enrolment JOIN Semesters ON Student_Enrolment.semester_id = Semesters.semester_id

sql:
SELECT DISTINCT s.semester_name
FROM Student_Enrolment se1
JOIN Degree_Programs dp1 ON se1.degree_program_id = dp1.degree_program_id
JOIN Semesters s ON se1.semester_id = s.semester_id
WHERE dp1.degree_summary_name = 'Master'
INTERSECT
SELECT DISTINCT s.semester_name
FROM Student_Enrolment se2
JOIN Degree_Programs dp2 ON se2.degree_program_id = dp2.degree_program_id
JOIN Semesters s ON se2.semester_id = s.semester_id
WHERE dp2.degree_summary_name = 'Bachelor'

inputs.question: What is the id of the semester that had both Masters and Bachelors students enrolled?

joins: Semesters JOIN Student_Enrolment ON Semesters.semester_id = Student_Enrolment.semester_id
Student_Enrolment JOIN Degree_Programs ON Student_Enrolment.degree_program_id = Degree_Programs.degree_program_id

sql:
SELECT DISTINCT s.semester_id
FROM Semesters s
JOIN Student_Enrolment se ON s.semester_id = se.semester_id
JOIN Degree_Programs dp ON se.degree_program_id = dp.degree_program_id
WHERE dp.degree_summary_name = 'Masters'
INTERSECT
SELECT DISTINCT s.semester_id
FROM Semesters s
JOIN Student_Enrolment se ON s.semester_id = se.semester_id
JOIN Degree_Programs dp ON se.degree_program_id = dp.degree_program_id
WHERE dp.degree_summary_name = 'Bachelors'

inputs.question: How many professionals did not operate any treatment on dogs?

joins: Treatments JOIN Professionals ON Treatments.professional_id = Professionals.professional_id
Treatments JOIN Dogs ON Treatments.dog_id = Dogs.dog_id
conditions: Professionals.professional_id NOT IN (SELECT Treatments.professional_id FROM Treatments JOIN Dogs ON Treatments.dog_id = Dogs.dog_id WHERE Dogs.dog_id IS NOT NULL)

sql:
SELECT COUNT(Professionals.professional_id)
FROM Professionals
WHERE Professionals.professional_id NOT IN (
    SELECT Treatments.professional_id
    FROM Treatments
    JOIN Dogs ON Treatments.dog_id = Dogs.dog_id
    WHERE Dogs.dog_id IS NOT NULL
)

Dogs JOIN Sizes ON Dogs.size_code = Sizes.size_code

sql:

FROM Dogs
